text: Quinazoline derivatives have been established as a significant class of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds possessing manifold pharmaceutical applications. This article aims at providing a thorough account of the recent advancements in the synthesis and evaluation of quinazoline derivatives along with an insight into their potential applications in medicine. The total literature, up to and including 2007, has been examined and categorized into five main strategies including the Aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated transformations, ultrasound-promoted methods and phase-transfer catalysis. Some supplementary strategies including oxidative cyclization, one-pot synthesis and reagent refluxing have also been reviewed. Besides these strategies, an overview of the diverse biological activities of quinazoline derivatives has also been made which reveal their anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and anti-obesity etc., properties. In all, an attempt has been made to consolidate the available data on structure activity relationships and on novel methods of synthesis that would serve as a basic reference for further progress in the design of potent drugs and pesticides.
